system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;

    }

    bottom
    {

    top

    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

boundaryField

    }

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

boundaryField

    bottom

        type            zeroGradient;

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;

    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 vertices (x = -0.15242 to 0)
    (-0.15242  0        -0.005)  // 0
    ( 0          0        -0.005)  // 1
    ( 0          0.1524   -0.005)  // 2
    (-0.15242  0.1524   -0.005)  // 3
    (-0.15242  0        0.005)   // 4
    ( 0          0        0.005)   // 5
    ( 0          0.1524   0.005)   // 6
    (-0.15242  0.1524   0.005)   // 7

    // Block 1 vertices (x = 0 to 0.1524)
    ( 0.1524  0        -0.005)  // 8
    ( 0.3048  0        -0.005)  // 9
    ( 0.3048  0.1524   -0.005)  // 10
    ( 0.1524  0.1524   -0.005)  // 11
    ( 0.1524  0        0.005)   // 12
    ( 0.3048  0        0.005)   // 13
    ( 0.3048  0.1524   0.005)   // 14
    ( 0.1524  0.1524   0.005)   // 15
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: hex (1 8 9 11 5 13 14 12)
    hex (1 8 9 11 5 13 14 12) (10 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: hex (2 9 10 11 6 13 14 15)
    hex (2 9 10 11 6 13 14 15) (10 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (9 14 15 13)

    }

    bottom

        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)

    }

    top

        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }

    wedge

        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
            (1 8 9 11)
            (2 9 10 11)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Analysis

### 1. `log.blockMesh` — Face Neighbor Issue

**Error:**  
```
face 0 in patch 1 does not have neighbour cell face: 4(9 14 15 13)
```

This is a **new error** compared to previous attempts. The face `(9 14 15 13)` is listed under the `outlet` patch. Let me analyze the current vertex layout:

- Vertex 9: `(0.3048, 0, -0.005)` — block 1
- Vertex 14: `(0.1524, 0.1524, 0.005)` — block 1
- Vertex 15: `(0.1524, 0, 0.005)` — block 1
- Vertex 13: `(0.3048, 0, 0.005)` — block 1

This face is entirely within **Block 1**, specifically the right-facing face of Block 1 (`hex (1 8 9 12 5 11 14 15)`). The error says this face has **no neighboring cell**, meaning there is no block adjacent to this face.

Looking at the block layout:
- Block 0: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` — occupies x ∈ [-0.15242, 0]
- Block 1: `hex (1 8 9 12 5 11 14 15)` — occupies x ∈ [0, 0.1524]
- Block 2: `hex (2 9 10 13 6 11 15 13)` — occupies x ∈ [0.1524, 0.3048]

The face `(9 14 15 13)` is the **right face** of Block 1. To its right (at x > 0.1524) should be **Block 2**, which shares this face. Indeed, Block 2 is defined as `hex (2 9 10 13 6 11 15 13)` — it shares the face `(9 13 15 14)` with Block 1. Wait — the orientation matters!

In OpenFOAM, face normals must be consistent. The face `(9 14 15 13)` in Block 1 has a normal pointing outward (+X direction). For Block 2 to be the neighbor, Block 2 must define the **opposite-oriented** face `(13 15 14 9)` as its left face. Let me verify Block 2's vertex ordering:

Block 2: `hex (2 9 10 13 6 11 15 13)` — this defines the block with vertices in a specific winding order. The face opposite vertex 1 (which is index 1 in the hex list) would be `(9 10 13 11)`. The face opposite vertex 5 (index 5) would be `(11 15 13 9)` — which is the reverse of `(9 13 15 11)`. 

The issue is likely a **winding/orientation mismatch** between the shared faces of Block 1 and Block 2. The face `(9 14 15 13)` in Block 1 needs to match the face `(13 15 14 9)` in Block 2 for them to be neighbors.

### Proposed Solution

**Fix the vertex ordering in Block 2** so that the shared face orientations match. Change Block 2 from:
```
hex (2 9 10 13 6 11 15 13)
```
to:
```
hex (2 13 10 9 6 15 11 13)
```

Or alternatively, change Block 1's outlet face definition from `(9 14 15 13)` to `(13 15 14 9)` to match Block 2's expected neighbor face orientation.

Actually, let me look more systematically. In a hex block `hex (a b c d e f g h)`, the face opposite vertex `b` is `(a c e g)` and the face opposite vertex `f` is `(h d b a)`. For Block 1: `hex (1 8 9 12 5 11 14 15)`:
- Opposite vertex 8 (index 1): face `(1 9 11 15)` — this is the left face (toward negative X)
- Opposite vertex 11 (index 5): face `(15 14 8 1)` — this is the right face (toward positive X)

So the right face of Block 1 is `(15 14 8 1)`, which corresponds to vertices `(9 13 15 11)` when remapped. Block 2 should have this as its left face. Block 2: `hex (2 9 10 13 6 11 15 13)` — opposite vertex 9 (index 1) gives face `(2 10 11 15)`, opposite vertex 11 (index 5) gives face `(13 15 9 2)`. Neither matches.

The cleanest fix: **change Block 1's outlet face** from `(9 14 15 13)` to `(15 14 8 1)` which is the actual right face of Block 1. Similarly, update the `outlet` patch to use the correct face.

### 2. `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Dependent on successful blockMesh

No direct action needed here.

---

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **In `blockMeshDict`**: Change the `outlet` patch face from `(9 14 15 13)` to `(15 14 8 1)` to match the actual right face of Block 1 and ensure it has a proper neighbor (Block 2).
2. **Ensure all boundary faces** use vertex orderings that correspond to the correct physical faces of their respective blocks.
3. After fixing blockMesh, `rhoCentralFoam` should run successfully since the mesh files (`points`, `faces`, etc.) will then be generated.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change outlet patch face from (9 14 15 13) to (15 14 8 1) to match the right face of Block 1 and ensure it has a proper neighbor cell in Block 2'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
